pitch: Cultivate fragile gardens across drifting asteroids before they tumble out of sunlight.
thesis: A timing-and-placement loop where light, drift, and scarce water force constant re-prioritisation.
player_fantasy: A patient gardener coaxing life from hostile rock.
replayability_hypothesis: Procedural asteroid drift and sunlight angles create different scarcity puzzles each run.

risk_register.risk: loop may resolve to one dominant planting order

first_playable_slice.scope: one asteroid, three plant types, sunlight sweep, 60s run

bot_success_criteria: random vs skilled bot score spread measurable
taste_gates: G1: is the primitive loop worth replaying?
kill_conditions: fails anti-boring gate after two deepen attempts
dominant planting order cannot be removed cheaply
